description: A trusted authserv-id reports DMARC pass and the evaluated header.from (example.com) matches the visible From domain, on an otherwise clean, all-passing message. dmarcHeaderFromDomains is [example.com] and dmarcHeaderFromMatchesFromDomain is true, so no dmarc.headerFromMismatch signal is emitted. Demonstrates the quiet DMARC-aligned baseline the consistency rule must not disturb.

authentication-results: mx.example.net; dmarc=pass header.from=example.com; spf=pass smtp.mailfrom=example.com; dkim=pass header.d=example.com

authenticationResults.raw: mx.example.net; dmarc=pass header.from=example.com; spf=pass smtp.mailfrom=example.com; dkim=pass header.d=example.com
